context_only: # tp v0.29.0 — Loop integrity: clean-round merge, close checkpoint, entry validation, excerpt parity

tasks.title: Move lock into .tp/locks
tasks.acceptance: Task-file lock lives at .tp/locks/<base>.lock covered by .tp/.gitignore, with a stale sibling <base>.tasks.json.lock removed on first write. tp init creates .tp/.gitignore, and tp commit or tp done --auto-commit refuse any --files path ending .tasks.json.lock.
tasks.closed_reason:
- Lock centralized under .tp/locks/<base>-<hash>.lock with stale sibling removed; .tp/.gitignore now covers locks/ (internal/engine/lock.go, internal/engine/projectconfig.go)
- tp init creates .tp/.gitignore at init time; tp commit and tp done --auto-commit refuse any --files path ending .tasks.json.lock (internal/cli/init.go, internal/cli/commit.go)

tasks.title: Retry write lock with backoff
tasks.acceptance: Write-lock acquisition retries with backoff until lock_timeout_seconds (default 5, range 1-60) elapses so two concurrent writes both succeed. On timeout tp exits 4 with a hint naming the lock path and elapsed wait.

tasks.title: Accept empty merge inputs
tasks.acceptance: --merge with all-empty inputs exits 0 and creates an empty -o file (merged_count 0), while a missing/unreadable input exits 3 and no input files exits 2. Malformed (invalid JSON) and incomplete (missing required fields) lines are skipped with a stderr warning without changing the exit code. Applies to both tp review --merge and tp audit --merge.
tasks.closed_reason:
- all-empty inputs exit 0 with a zero-byte -o file and merged_count 0; a missing/unreadable input exits 3 and zero input files exit 2 (§3.1, §3.3 rows 2,4-6)
- malformed (invalid JSON) and incomplete (missing required fields) lines are skipped with a stderr warning naming which, without changing the exit code (§3.2, §3.3 row 3)
- identical fix applied to both tp review --merge (review_merge.go) and tp audit --merge (audit_merge.go), each covered by its own test suite

tasks.title: Scope mode positionals
tasks.acceptance: tp review/audit --merge rejects a spec positional among its inputs with exit 2, and --resolve or --resolve-all take the findings NDJSON as positional where a .md or spec-looking positional exits 2 naming the expected form. --resolve indices are 0-based and stated in usage, --help, and merged output. A non-numeric index exits 2 naming the expected form.

tasks.title: Validate tp add at entry
tasks.acceptance: tp add rejects each section 6.1 rule (missing/blank/whitespace id, duplicate id, missing/blank title, missing/blank acceptance, no source anchor, depends_on unknown id, invalid JSON) with the stated exit (1, or 2 for invalid JSON) and a hint. Every task tp writes carries [] not null for depends_on, source_sections, and tags; tp lint structured_elements.tables/numbered_lists and tp stats tags are [] when empty.
tasks.closed_reason:
- tp add rejects all seven §6.1 entry rules at entry (exit 1; exit 2 for invalid JSON with the decoder detail in hint, not error) via shared engine.ValidateTaskEntry called by both tp add and tp import; --bulk/--stdin validates after staging the whole batch so a task may depends_on a later task in the same batch
- every task tp writes carries [] not null for depends_on, source_sections, and tags — WriteTaskFile normalizes nil slices and the Task tags omitempty was dropped
- tp lint structured_elements.tables/numbered_lists and tp stats tags serialize as [] when empty (initialized empty in CheckStructuredElements and runStats)

tasks.title: Compute coverage on every write
tasks.acceptance: AutoFillCoverage runs after add, import, remove, and set of source_sections/source_lines whenever the spec is readable, so tp init + tp add + tp validate is clean with no import round trip. When the spec cannot be read the coverage block is left untouched and tp validate's coverage finding hints the unreadable spec path.
source_sections: ## 7. Coverage is computed on every write
tasks.closed_reason:
- AutoFillCoverage now runs after add/remove/set (and import) whenever the spec is readable, so tp init + tp add + tp validate is clean with no import round-trip.
- When the spec is unreadable the coverage block is left untouched and tp validate's coverage finding carries a hint naming the spec path.

tasks.title: Emit spec_excerpt from source_sections
tasks.acceptance: A task with source_sections and no source_lines carries the section text (heading + body to next same/shallower heading, in listed order, blank-line-joined) in spec_excerpt for tp plan, tp show, tp next, and tp next --peek, capped at 2000 chars with the existing marker. --compact omits spec_excerpt and, per 8.4, keeps decision-critical new fields (bookkeeping, suggested_files, max_rounds/rounds_remaining/in_flight_round) while omitting explanatory ones (skipped_roles, attribution_excludes, audit overlap_report, report note).
tasks.closed_reason:
- spec_excerpt now assembled from source_sections when source_lines is absent: each entry contributes its heading line plus the body through the next same/shallower-level heading, blank-line joined and capped at 2000 chars (ExtractSpecExcerptForTask in engine/excerpt.go)
- parity verified across plan, show, next, and next --peek; --compact omits spec_excerpt entirely (engine + cli tests green, golangci-lint clean)

tasks.title: Fold closure into the commit
tasks.acceptance: Under commit_strategy builtin, tp commit and tp done --auto-commit fold the closure into the implementation commit so the committed task file shows status:done and git status is clean for tp-owned paths. commit_sha or commit_shas[0] records the pre-amend implementation sha C1 (never the post-amend object sha), and suggested_files reads C1's diff. The amend runs only when HEAD is still C1 and the working-tree diff lists only tp-written paths, else a follow-up commit chore(tp): record <id> closure leaves C1 as commit_sha.
tasks.closed_reason:
- commit-then-close-then-amend folds status:done into C2; committed task file shows done, working tree clean for tp-owned paths (commit.go runCommit + done.go runDoneSingle)
- commit_sha records the pre-amend implementation sha C1, never the post-amend C2; verified recorded sha != amended HEAD across fold and fallback paths
- amend guarded on HEAD==C1 and tp-written-only working-tree diff; else follow-up chore(tp): record <id> closure leaves C1 as commit_sha (tests 5527e01 26ad1e8)

tasks.title: Report bookkeeping in resume
tasks.acceptance: Under commit_strategy hc, tp resume reports modified tp-owned files (the task file, .tp-review/, .tp/) in a bookkeeping array with {path, kind, ref} (closure/round/config), separate from changes and raising no unexplained-changes blocker; the array is [] when no tp-owned file is dirty. tp resume emits an execution-model guidance note when phase is implement, telling the driver to run each unit in a fresh subagent or context to preserve context hygiene. Both bookkeeping and the guidance note survive --compact per 8.4.

tasks.title: State skipped roles
tasks.acceptance: tp review and tp audit prompt emission lists every non-emitted corpus role in skipped_roles [{role, reason}] with reason one of no-checklist-items, no-spec-change, domain-mismatch, or no-baseline ([] when none skipped). Merge and --status output add attribution_excludes: ["regression"] only when that exclusion causes merged_count to exceed the overlap-report finding count, and tp audit --merge or --status emit overlap_report over non-PASS rows clustered by (item_id, category). skipped_roles, attribution_excludes, and audit overlap_report are omitted under --compact per 8.4.

tasks.title: Surface in-flight round state
tasks.acceptance: --status reports max_rounds (null when uncapped) and rounds_remaining, writes snapshot-round-N.md atomically at round start and review-round-N.ndjson at record, and reports in_flight_round N (null when none) with tp resume next_action = record-round N. The regression prompt names its baseline snapshot path (snapshot-round-<N-1>.md), and round 1 adds regression to skipped_roles with reason no-baseline. max_rounds, rounds_remaining, and in_flight_round survive --compact per 8.4.

tasks.title: Suggest audit files from commits
tasks.acceptance: When tp audit finds no audit-able file it exits 4 with suggested_files: the union of paths touched by every SHA in commit_shas of done tasks (type-filtered), [] when none, and a hint naming --affected-files/--affected-from-tasks. tp audit --affected-from-tasks audits that derived set directly. suggested_files survives --compact per 8.4.
tasks.closed_reason:
- exit-4 payload carries suggested_files: the type-filtered union of paths touched by every SHA in commit_shas of done tasks ([] when none, e.g. covered-by)
- tp audit --affected-from-tasks audits that derived file set directly, bypassing diff auto-detection
- suggested_files is decision-critical and survives --compact per §8.4

tasks.title: Fix non-conformant exit codes
tasks.acceptance: Each section 13.1 case returns exit 2: invalid-JSON add with decoder detail in hint, cobra flag-parse failure as a tp error object, and a dash-leading done reason with a hint naming the -- separator. Every non-zero error object carries a hint naming the next command to run rather than just the rule broken.

tasks.title: Round report durations correctly
tasks.acceptance: tp report rounds actual_minutes to one decimal, and when it rounds to 0.0 the accuracy is null with note "duration below resolution". The summary excludes null-accuracy tasks from estimation_accuracy and reports how many were excluded, while the report note is omitted under --compact per 8.4.
tasks.closed_reason:
- actual_minutes rounds to 1 dp; sub-resolution duration (rounds to 0.0) yields accuracy null + note "duration below resolution"; summary excludes it from estimation_accuracy and reports excluded_from_accuracy (internal/cli/report.go computeReport)
- --compact omits the report note per section 8.4 (internal/cli/report.go runReport); covered by TestReportSubResolutionDuration, TestReportAllExcludedAccuracy, TestReportCompactOmitsNote

tasks.title: Skip container headings in lint
tasks.acceptance: empty-section does not fire on a container heading (whose next heading is deeper than itself) but still fires on an empty leaf heading.
tasks.closed_reason: - empty-section now skips container headings (next heading deeper than itself) in internal/engine/lint_rules.go:CheckEmptySections; empty leaf headings still error; covered by TestCheckEmptySections in internal/engine/lint_test.go

tasks.title: Set commit_strategy at project level
tasks.acceptance: tp set --workflow --project commit_strategy=<builtin|auto|hc> writes workflow.commit_strategy in .tp/config.json participating in resolve-at-read order, and tp config --resolved annotates commit_strategy with its source layer. tp set --workflow commit_strategy=... (task level) still exits 2 with a hint naming the project-level setter.
tasks.closed_reason:
- project commit_strategy settable via --project, writes workflow.commit_strategy in .tp/config.json, participates in resolve-at-read order (d0610e7)
- config --resolved annotates commit_strategy with its source layer {value,source} (32762e9, 51fc20d)
- task-level set --workflow commit_strategy keeps exit-2 refusal, hint names project setter (94511b1, 79cdb83)

tasks.title: Update docs for all changes
tasks.acceptance: SKILL.md documents driving tp from a non-Claude runtime (sub-agent fan-out, orchestrator injection duty, permission-prompt rule), and README.md with REFERENCE.md reflect every section 3-16 flag, field, and behavior. CLAUDE.md's QA recipe passes tp lint and tp validate, and go test ./... with golangci-lint run are clean.
